examples.title: Upgrade from object to Lock
examples.description: Swapping a plain `object` sentinel for `System.Threading.Lock` keeps the familiar `lock` call-site syntax while giving the runtime and compiler a richer type to optimize against.

examples.title: RAII-style scoped entry with EnterScope
examples.description: `Lock.EnterScope()` returns a disposable scope, so you can protect a multi-step transaction with a `using` statement and guarantee release even when exceptions occur.

relatedFeatures.reason: Start with Async and await for foundational async patterns before reaching for Lock in concurrent code.
Continue to Async Streams when you need safe producer/consumer pipelines alongside locking.
Explore Ref Structs to understand the stack-allocated value that EnterScope() returns under the hood.
